value: Initial draft; finance section to be completed by Ministry of Finance.

narrative: Indonesia has a National Disaster Management Plan (Renas PB) covering response and early recovery. A standalone recovery framework is in draft and has been used in Central Sulawesi (2018) and Lombok (2018). Roles are defined for BNPB at national level and BPBD at sub-national level. Coordination with line ministries is established but operates inconsistently between events.
evidence: BNPB Regulation No. 5/2017; Renas PB 2020-2024; Central Sulawesi Recovery Master Plan 2019; interview with BNPB Recovery Directorate, March 2026.

narrative: Recovery readiness is not formally assessed on a regular cycle. Post-event reviews are conducted after major disasters but findings are not systematically fed back into framework revision. A gap analysis was conducted in 2022 but recommendations were partially implemented.
evidence: BNPB internal review 2022; AHA Centre regional readiness assessment 2023.

narrative: Most line ministries do not have formal recovery strategies. Public Works has an internal reconstruction guideline but it is not a recovery strategy in the sense of this indicator. Health and Education sectors operate from response-focused contingency plans only.

Recovery is referenced in RPJMN (national medium-term development plan) and the National DRR Plan. Climate adaptation is integrated through the long-term low carbon and climate resilience strategy. Coordination is uneven in practice.

The Ministry of Education and Culture maintains a heritage register but post-disaster protection arrangements are unclear. Need to consult further with the Directorate General of Culture before finalizing.
DEFER — Ministry of Health to complete this section.

Disaster Management Law (Law 24/2007) provides general legal basis but recovery-specific provisions are limited. Government Regulation 21/2008 elaborates implementation. There is no permanent legal basis for fast-tracking procurement during recovery; ad-hoc Presidential Instructions have been issued after major events.
evidence: Law 24/2007 on Disaster Management; PP 21/2008; Presidential Instruction 8/2018 (Sulawesi).

narrative: BNPB has established mechanisms for capacity-building and surge support to BPBD at provincial and district levels. Pre-agreed support arrangements exist for major events; routine support varies by region.
evidence: BNPB Strategic Plan 2020-2024; BPBD capacity assessment 2024.

narrative: Pending input from Ministry of Finance — Directorate of Risk Financing.
Emergency management training is delivered through BNPB's training centre and several universities. Recovery-specific content is limited; most courses focus on response and preparedness.
evidence: BNPB training catalogue 2026; Universitas Pertahanan curriculum.

narrative: Statistics Indonesia (BPS) maintains baseline socioeconomic data at sub-district level. Damage and loss data is collected through the DIBI system. Integration between BPS and DIBI is improving but not automated.
evidence: BPS Statistical Yearbook; DIBI online database; MoU between BPS and BNPB (2023).

narrative: DIBI system tracks economic losses for major events. Non-economic loss tracking is not systematic. Sendai Framework Monitor reporting is conducted but data quality varies.
evidence: DIBI system documentation; SFM national reports 2018-2024.
